reasoning: The control SC-7 (5) focuses on boundary protection with a deny-by-default and allow-by-exception approach. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to network security configurations that restrict access by default and only allow specific, defined traffic. NetworkPolicies can enforce default deny rules for both ingress and egress traffic, ensuring that no traffic is allowed unless explicitly permitted. Additionally, restricting the use of LoadBalancer services and ensuring TLS is enabled for Ingress resources align with the principle of minimizing public exposure and securing access.
title: Boundary Protection | Deny by Default — Allow by Exception
